details.service_description: Submerged, fine bubble, diffused jet aeration systems that utilize the ejector method of contacting gases and liquids. The system features double nozzle arrangements with a primary inner nozzle, intermediate high shear mixing chamber, and outer secondary nozzle. Oxygen transfer occurs through multiple zones: high contact zone within nozzles creating micro-size bubbles (0.1 to 1 mm diameter), high pressure zone at tank bottom with horizontal plume travel, buoyancy bubble rise (1 to 3 mm diameter), and surface agitation. Applications include gas/liquid contacting, blending, and solids suspension. The system is more effective than other aeration methods because it utilizes multiple oxygen transfer zones. All aeration and mixing occurs below the surface, eliminating mist or spray problems and icing issues. Available in several configurations including Directional Mix Jet Aerators (DMJA), Bidirectional Mix Jet Aerators (BMJA), and Eddy Mix Jet Aerators (EMJA). Serves industries including pulp and paper, textile, chemical, petrochemical and refinery, pharmaceutical, food and beverage, corn processing, and municipal. Installations worldwide including locations in Canada, USA, Europe, and Indonesia.
